observations.recommendation:
1.20 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to record adjustments at year end for unreleased
checks and to restore stale checks to the Cash in Bank and payable
accounts;
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the
depository bank for the rectification of bank errors; and
c. Municipal Accountant to use the ledger balance of the Cash in Bank
account for the balance per book in the preparation of the BRS.

1.21 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of records to determine the unreconciled balances, so that
discrepancies can be immediately traced and necessary adjusting
entries be made accordingly;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for
valid reconciling items per books such as bank memos, interest, errors
and other unrecorded adjustments; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees or holder in
due course to surrender the stale checks for cancellation and reissuance
of a new one;

2.13 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following:
a. Municipal Treasurer to submit a duly certified and updated copies of
cash book on all funds as of December 31, 2024;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to implement
periodic reconciliation of accounts to promptly identify the
discrepancies and make necessary adjustments to accordingly correct
the balances; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cashbooks for Cash Local
Treasury and Cash in Bank, LCCA Accounts, per fund, as required by
MNGAS for LGUs.

3.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the GSO and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update
the Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively;
b. Direct the GSO and Accounting Office to fully reconcile the PPE
accounts recorded in the books to establish the reliability of the
balances of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements;
c. Instruct the Inventory Committee to prepare a complete RPCPPE, and
the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) in the conduct and completion of the
physical inventory and refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the
conduct of the next physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment
and the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

4.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count of PPE
and include all the PPE in the preparation of the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), ensure completeness
of property number tags for each PPE and submit the same to the Office of
the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

5.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide copies of the
report of completed projects with necessary supporting documents to
the Office of the Municipal Accountant; to coordinate and regularly
reconcile their records to determine the updated status and correct
balances of the CIP accounts;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the affected projects,
whose progress payments were erroneously recorded to Construction
in Progress – Buildings and Other Structures instead of Construction in
Progress – Infrastructure Assets; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the identified
completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts and to record the
related accumulated depreciation and the corresponding depreciation
expense.

6.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount of RPT and
SET receivables per books based on the certified list of Real Property
Taxpayers from the Treasurer’s Office and make the necessary journal
entries to adjust the receivable accounts; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant
at the beginning of the year a duly certified list of collectibles for the
year which shall be the basis of the latter to set-up the correct amount
of RPT/SET Receivables to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume I
of the MNGAS for LGUs.

7.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Maintain RAAOs for the LDRRMF current year appropriation and
separate ledgers to record all transfers of unutilized DRRMF to the
special trust fund by year;
b. Reconcile the balances of the LDRRM Fund with the General and Trust
Fund books; and
c. Coordinate with the LDRRMO in monitoring and reconciling the
balance of the LDRRM Fund;

8.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Concerned implementing offices/departments to expedite
implementation of various PPAs funded by NGAs and other LGUs;
b. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Exert best effort to locate the documents related to the non-moving
balances and ascertain the reasons why they have remained in the
books for an extended period;
2. Coordinate with the Municipality’s implementing
offices/departments to identify the source agencies so that excess
amount can be returned or remitted; and
3. Verify, analyze and reconcile the undetermined balances posted in
the Subsidiary Ledger in order to present accurate balances of the
Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs account in the financial statements.
c. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately
return or remit the excess amount from the completed projects to the
identified source agencies, or request written authority to utilize the
remaining amount for other priority projects of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the: a. Municipal MDRRMO Officer and the Municipal Accountant to: a.1 ensure that only transactions with valid claims and covered with proper contracts and documents evidencing receipt of goods and services are recognized as obligated/ utilized in the financial records; a.2 establish a sound system of internal control in the recording and monitoring of obligations to make certain it is properly recognized in the books, and the balances are correctly presented in the financial reports. b. Municipal Accountant to record and transfer the remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund.

Municipal Accountant to record and transfer the remaining unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count of PPE and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;

Direct the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to update the Property and Ledger Cards and fully reconcile the PPE accounts recorded in the books with the report on actual physical count to establish the reliability of the balances of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements; and
Refer to 
COA Circular 
No. 2020-006 in the conduct of the next physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment.  This circular is issued for one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; thus, in no case shall the herein procedures be used to further derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs and/or cleanse subsequent discrepancies or unreconciled balances in PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate, timely submit the necessary documents to support valid reconciling items and record the appropriate adjustments in the BRS in order to fairly present the balances of Cash in Bank Accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to review Real Property valuations and assessments, update and reconcile the balance of real property tax due, and apply necessary adjustments in the books, if any, to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements enumerated in Section 21 of RA No. 10212, 
Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM- DILG JMC 
No. 2013-1 and 
Section 4 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC 
No. 2014-1 by prioritizing disaster management related projects and charge the office/ administrative related expenses against the MOOE and CO of the General Fund.

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate and obtain the necessary documents to support valid reconciling items and make appropriate adjustments in the BRS to fairly present the balances of Cash in Bank Accounts in the Financial Statements;

Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to verify and monitor all check releases and ensure the proper presentation and classification of outstanding checks in the Bank Reconciliation Statements; and

Municipal Accountant to retrieve the original copy of checks and its supporting documents for all verified stale checks in order to facilitate the preparation of adjusting entries to correct the balance of Cash in Bank.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances whose purpose has already been served and strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular 
No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We also recommended the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold the dues to all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to verify any unconsumed inventory and provide necessary adjustments as required in COA Circular No. 2014-002 to ensure the accuracy and fairness of balances presented in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the guidelines in the implementation of LGS-F AM projects of the Municipality in accordance with not only the Government Procurement Reform Act but also those required in the Memorandum Circular issued relative to the project.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure that before payment, all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign personnel to process the Certificate of Title of the lands under the name of the Municipality and ensure that these are registered under the Torrens Title System to ascertain ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality over the properties.



We also recommended that proper reconciliation between the records of the Accounting Office and Assessor’s Office be made so that the Land account is recorded completely, valued correctly, and presented accurately in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit fully and promptly the inter-agency payables to the concerned government agencies in compliance with the above-mentioned laws and regulations.
We recommended the submission of additional documents that could provide more details so that the exact amount to be refunded can be identified.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform the concerned official and employee to submit the duly approved leave application and justification on monetizing more than 50% of the accumulated leave credits.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to:
• provide him with the necessary documents enumerated under COA Circular
No. 2016-005 and attach the same for the filing of the request for authority to
write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team; and
• in the meantime, set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to
present fairly the Receivables account in the financial statements at net
realizable value consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005
and for the fair presentation of the account in the financial statements as
required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Acting City Treasurer to request
the depository banks to provide timely the bank statements or snap shot as of
cut-off date which will serve as a preliminary statement to validate any
discrepancies; and instruct the assigned personnel to implement strict monitoring
to avoid checks of becoming stale and ensure that notice is given to every payee
whose check is about to become stale at least one month before expiry.

Thereafter, the OIC-City Accountant should ensure that BRS are prepared timely
and any reconciling items are adjusted promptly in the books of accounts for the
fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements as
required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to request
from the SP the authority to revert the total balance of P3,179,297.71 to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the GF with a condition that appropriation shall be
made available in the event that claim arises in the future and in compliance with
the requirement of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 for the fair presentation of the
account in the financial statements.

Henceforth, as part of the internal control of the City, ensure that there is proper
monitoring of the bidders bond/retention money so that claims nearing the
maturity of two years, are communicated to the respective suppliers/contractors
for the processing of their claims by submitting the necessary documents. In the
event that no response is received from the suppliers/contractors, prepare the
schedule and make a request for authority to revert to the Unappropriated Surplus
of the GF from the SP. Thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entries in the
books of accounts to fairly present the account balance in the financial
statements in accordance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

present the account balance in the financial statements in accordance with
Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

observations.managementAction: Confirmations had been made by the OIC-City Accountant to various creditors and no claims were received by her office which prompted the passage of a Resolution by the Sangguniang Panlungsod, duly approved by the City Mayor for the reversion. The OIC-City Accountant commented that she already reverted the amount of P2,855,111.27 to the Unappropriated Surplus on February 1, 2020, and a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for such reversion was submitted to the Audit Team on July 15, 2020.
observations.reason: The reversion of the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF was not yet processed by the OIC-City Accountant due to voluminous workload. A balance of P68,807.59 is still for reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to transfer the amount of P151,477.25 to the GF, and henceforth, comply with DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant commented that the excess collections on the sale of bidding documents were already transferred to the GF per Check No. 34141104 dated February 5, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to confirm with the creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims. In the event they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod through a Resolution with the condition that appropriation shall be made in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant commented that the amount of P2,653,159.00 was already reverted to Unappropriated Surplus. However, the amount of P465,972.89 is still for reversion pending the request from the Sangguniang Panlungsod for its reversion.
observations.reason: Awaiting the Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution authorizing the reversion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to assign personnel who will monitor the checks and to send written notices to the payees whose checks are about to become stale at least one month before expiry. If no response was received within the given time, effect the cancellation of the stale check; and request the OIC-City Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the City’s books of accounts by debiting Cash in Bank account and crediting Prior Period Adjustment account for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer commented that the amount due to Smart Communications, Inc. amounting to P9,020.61 was already settled by the City Mayor, hence the check issued was not used and become stale. The Disbursement Voucher (DV) for the adjustment was already forwarded to the Accounting Office. Upon inquiry with the payee of the two checks totaling P23,141.00, the same could not be found despite diligent efforts by the payee and are already stale, hence an affidavit of loss was requested. For stale checks issued under Trust Fund, the City Treasurer made series of calls to notify the payees that the checks were already stale but there was no action on their part, thus the checks remain outstanding and stale. The City Treasurer effected the cancellation of the stale checks and requested the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to: pursue the collection of the uncollected amount totaling P2,750.24 from the beneficiaries who are still in service, otherwise, deduct it from their salaries or from whatever money due them; send demand letters to the separated employees for their respective loan balances totaling P218,300.39, otherwise, prepare the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and file the same with the Commission on Audit; and process the refunds to the beneficiaries with excess payments totaling P3,851.46.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer commented that as of May 14, 2020, all those beneficiaries who are in service and two persons who are separated in service settled their accounts totaling P2,750.24 and P4,473.24, respectively. Meanwhile, for others who are separated in service, assistance was sought from the HRMO to provide the last known address based on Personal Data Sheet on file and Local Civil Registrar as some were alleged already deceased. Moreover, payroll for refund of overpayment have been prepared and processed except for those who are nowhere to be found.
observations.reason: There are difficulties in locating the new addresses of the separated employees to send the demand letters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to return immediately the unutilized funds totaling P1,750,000.00 to the source agency and the P1,322,455.14 representing the unexpended balance from completed projects to the Bureau of Treasury so that it could be used in other projects or by other agencies needing financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant commented that they have already processed the transfer of funds to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer is still gathering the documents to support the return of the unutilized funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-City Accountant: trace and retrieve the pertinent documents relative to the balances already made in the accounts; prepare the necessary adjustments, if it becomes futile to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and adopt an adequate internal control system with regard to the collection and its remittance to ensure accuracy and reliability of accounting the collections and remittances.
observations.managementAction: Documents were gathered to support the adjustments already made in the books of accounts. The necessary adjustments were prepared and already made in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, through the OIC-City Assessor, submit the proposed schedule of fair market values to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for its appropriate action, and effect the approved general revision of real property assessments as required by law.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Assessor commented that their office had made several attempts for the approval of the revised schedule of real property values way back in 2012. However, for the meantime, updating of fair values and tax mapping of the real properties within the City is continuously being prepared to serve as an initial base line information for the tax impact study that will serve as the starting point for a new proposed schedule of fair market values.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Panlungsod has not acted on the proposed schedule; the OIC-City Assessor continues to prepare baseline data for a new proposed schedule.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor: instruct the OIC-City Engineer to submit the list of all ongoing PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year in January of every year, providing the information as required under Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted for validation.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Engineer committed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The OIC-City Engineer failed to prepare and submit the reports; the OIC-City Engineer only submitted the Quarterly Report for the 1st Quarter of 2019 and did not submit subsequent reports as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the book balance of the municipality and the records of Municipal Development Fund Office of the Department of Finance and record any necessary adjustments after the reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to trace up the unreconciled balance of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make necessary adjustment, where appropriate, to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial operation as of a given period of time.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of charging the wages of job orders and other inappropriate expenses to the Special Education Fund and adhere to the guidelines provided in the DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017. We further recommended that these expenditures be charged to the General Fund of the Municipality and expenses related to Alternative Learning System (ALS) must be charged to the funds provided by the Department of Education.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unutilized balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and record the transfer to the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books at the end of the year. We also recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the guidelines set by COA Circular No.2012-002 dated September 2, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all officials concerned to maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for implementation of various projects, programs and activities to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDO/local development council to ensure that the fund be budgeted and expended in accordance with the proper utilization guidelines of the 20% Development Fund and to establish guidelines for monitoring and evaluation of projects. We also recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist submit the documents relative to the livestock livelihood program and closely monitor the said projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned appropriate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment in the Annual Budget for the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities of Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) in order to cater the needs and uphold the rights and interests of the children in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the DILG Operations Field Officer assigned to the LGU and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to assist the Municipal Planning Development Office (MPDO) in the generation and processing of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information to complement the GAD database. Likewise, LGU GFPS shall assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code or ordinance. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the M&E Team. Further, the municipality adhere to the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women provided by the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 and any updates concerning GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned observe strictly the rules, regulations and guidelines in LGU transfers/advances to LEEs/PUs and consider the options provided in Item 3.4 of LBC No. 100 in the case of its Municipal Economic Enterprise (MEE) operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned observe strictly the rules, regulations and guidelines specifically provided in appropriating expenses for Personal Services. We also recommended the refund of benefits received in excess of PS Limitation amounting to ₱576,954.34.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: Reclassify the fund transfer from Other MOOE to Due from other LGUs; Monitor and require the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs; Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations. We also recommended that the MPDO submit copy of the agreed policy and procedures in the monitoring of PAPs and the corresponding report thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor request the:
 Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass a Resolution authorizing him to file the
request for authority to write-off of dormant receivables; and
 OIC-City Accountant to facilitate the documents needed to support the request
for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and
procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City
Accountant to confirm with the account owners/creditors and to request them to
submit documents pertaining to their claims. In the event that they fail to support
their claims, revert the account to the unappropriated surplus of the GF, subject to
an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation
shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to transfer
the amount of P151,477.25 to the GF and henceforth, comply with DBM Budget
Circular No. 2004-5A.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to confirm
with the creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their
claims. In the event they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the GF subject to an authority from the Sangguniang

made in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provisions of
Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

are about to become stale at least one month before expiry. If no response was
received within the given time, effect the cancellation of the stale check; and
 request the OIC-City Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the City’s
books of accounts by debiting Cash in Bank account and crediting Prior Period

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to:
 pursue the collection of the uncollected amount totaling P60,445.20 from the
beneficiaries who are still in service, otherwise, deduct it from their salaries or
from whatever money due them;
 send demand letters to the separated employees for their respective loan
balances totaling P218,300.39, otherwise, prepare the request for authority to

under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and file the same with the Commission on
Audit; and
 process the refunds to the beneficiaries the excess payments totaling P3,851.46.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to return
immediately the unutilized funds totaling P1,750,000.00 to the source agency and
the P1,322,455.14 representing the unexpended balance from completed projects
to the Bureau of Treasury so that it could be used in other projects or by other
agencies needing financial assistance.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to:
 trace and retrieve the pertinent documents relative to the balances since year
2013 and prepare the necessary adjustments, if it becomes futile to revert the
account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, pursuant to the
provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
 adopt an adequate internal control system with regard to the collection made by
the City Treasurer for other government units and its remittance to ensure
accuracy and reliability of accounting the collections and remittances.

We recommended that the City Mayor through the City Assessor’s Office submit
the proposed schedule of fair market values to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for its
appropriate action as required by law.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
 instruct the OIC-City Engineer to submit the list of all ongoing PPA and those
that are to be implemented during the year in January of every year providing
the information as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
 inform the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure
project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project
signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  Municipal  Accountant,
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the complete  set
of financial statements and necessary reports within the  prescribed  period
for the Auditor to be able to validate and examine if necessary  adjustments
will be made and to determine on what opinion to be rendered.

We recommended that the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant  and
Equipment (RPCPPE) be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later  than
   January 31 of each year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and GSO reconcile the  PPE
accounts recorded in the books with the inventory report on actual  physical
count to establish the correctness and reliability of the  balances  of  PPE
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer update the cashbook daily  based
on  the  Report  of  Collections  and  Deposits  (RCD)  submitted   by   the
Liquidating Officer and/or collecting officers upon remittance  and  closely
monitor the cash balance by reconciling daily the  cash  on  hand  with  the
cash balance in the cashbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a separate cubicle  for  the
Liquidating/ Disbursing  Officer.   Such  partition  must  be  appropriately
enclosed and given corresponding lock. The Liquidating/  Disbursing  Officer
must ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed  to  have  access  to
the room in order to protect government funds and property against  risk  of
loss thru theft.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal  Treasurer  and
OIC-Municipal Accountant to  reconcile  account  with  the  records  of  the
Province of Iloilo and make  necessary  adjusting  entries  to  reflect  the
correct balance of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all  officials  concerned  to
maximize  the  utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  allocated   for
implementation of various projects, programs and activities to help  achieve
the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes  of  the
Municipality.


We also recommended the review of prior years’ DF  and  revert  savings,  if
there are any, from completed projects pursuant to Section 322 of  R.A.  No.
7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the  LDRRMO  to  prioritize
the projects and activities enumerated in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and  DILG
JMC     No. 2013-1 like procurement of basic rescue and  response  equipment
necessary  for  disaster  preparedness  and  disaster  response  and  charge
administrative expenses to the MOOE and CO of the general fund of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal  Solid  Waste
Management Board to adopt specific revenue-generating measures and look  for
other  sources  of  revenue  to   ensure   the   viability   and   effective
implementation of programs, projects and activities included in its  10-year
Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
initiate  effective  measures  to  collect  monthly  rentals  from   market
stallholders and strictly impose the  penalties  for  late  or  non-payment
thereof. Demand letters should be  issued  to  delinquent  stallholders  to
formally remind them of their dues with the Municipality and  to  institute
legal sanctions, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all  officials  concerned  to
refrain  from  purchasing  goods  and  services  through  reimbursement   in
compliance with the pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of RA  No.  9184
and withhold the corresponding creditable withholding taxes and final value-
added taxes due to the BIR.

We recommended that the Local School Board refrain  from  charging  expenses
related to the implementation of  programs  and  activities  of  Alternative
Learning System (ALS) against SEF which instead should be sourced  out  from
the funds provided by the Department of Education.

We recommended that the Local School Board follow the  proper  planning  and
budgeting  procedures  for  the  SEF  to  ensure  strategic  and   efficient
utilization of  fund  intended  for  the  priority  programs,  projects  and
activities of various schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  concerned  department
heads  to  submit  the  duly  accomplished  AAPSI on  each   findings   and
recommendations discussed in the CY 2017 Annual Audit  Report  pertinent  to
their offices, in compliance with Section 88 of the  General  Provisions  of
RA No. 10924, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2017.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC - General Services Officer to complete the details of properties in the RPCPPE by securing copies of Unserviceable Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) and comply with the procedural and documentary requirements for the inclusion of property disposal.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-General Services Officer submitted the RPCPPE as of year-end as well as the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which will serve as the basis for the Accounting Office to drop from the books of accounts unserviceable properties already disposed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to prepare the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and file the same to the COA.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor filed the request
observations.reason: The request for authority to write-off is pending action of the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: Not Awaiting the Sangguniang Panlungsod resolution authorizing the reversion.
observations.reason: (Reiterated in audit observation no. 2, page no. 53)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to transfer the amount P583,712.75 to the General Fund and henceforth, comply with DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P583,712.75 representing excess collections from the sale of bidding documents for CY 2017 was eventually transferred to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to review the Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) of Cash in Bank - LCCA by tracing the cause/s of abnormal balances totaling P72,536,883.11 and the City Treasurer to maintain separate bank account for each special account to facilitate monitoring of the cash balance.
observations.managementAction: The SLs of Cash in Bank - LCCA were already adjusted reflecting the correct balances and the City Treasurer maintained separate bank accounts for each special account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Planning and Development Coordinator and Development Project Coordinator to monitor the implementation of the projects, if still feasible. We also recommended to return immediately the excess funds of P1,322,455.14 to the Bureau of Treasury and the fund of P1,000,000.00 for cancelled project to the BTr.
observations.managementAction: The City Planning and Development Coordinator and Development Coordinator was partially implemented BUB implemented due to lack of feasibility study and other legal matters; and lack of coordination between end-users and focal persons. We also Not The City Treasurer did not process the return of the unutilized funds to the BTr. (Reiterated in audit observation no.7 with revision on the amount to be returned to the BTr, page no. 59)
observations.reason: Coordinator was partially implemented due to lack of feasibility study and other legal matters; and lack of coordination between end-users and focal persons.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) or the designated program coordinator to monitor the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF to ensure timely completion; and City Budget Officer to provide specific or the itemized projects in the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations, and Balances of the 20% component of Internal Revenue Allotment.
observations.managementAction: The City implemented all projects under the 20% DF except the installation of electrical facilities and construction of drainage due to the right of way issues between the City government and the private owner.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Officer (LDRRMO) to monitor the implementation of the projects that remain unimplemented and take action on the cause/s of the delay or the OIC-City Accountant to submit immediately the required documents for the disbursements amounting to P1,581,620.00 for post-audit and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant to submit immediately the required documents for the disbursements amounting to P1,581,620.00 and required documents for disbursements were submitted. The OIC-City Accountant submitted the DVs and supporting documents to the Audit Team within considerable days after due date and the Audit Team found them complete.
observations.reason: Delay in submission of disbursement vouchers and incomplete documentation caused initial inability to ascertain regularity; supporting documents later submitted and found complete by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor support the OIC-City Assessor to cause the preparation of a Tax Impact Study that will be the basis of the revised schedule of fair market value for mass appraisal of real property units of the City. The sooner the revision is done, the sooner the City to realize additional income from the expected increase in real property tax collection.
observations.managementAction: Continuous update of the fair market values of the real properties is done and the necessary funds for the processing of the general revision was also requested for 2019. Moreover, tax impact study showing the assessment levels and values will be made and be submitted to the Sangguniang Panlungsod as a basis for the general revision of the real property assessments.
observations.reason: Implemented partially due to inaction of the Sangguniang Panlungsod on the submitted schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) or designated personnel to ensure that complete reports, accomplishment reports, programs/projects are indicated in the payrolls and appointments are supported with statements of duties and responsibilities of the personnel to facilitate validation of their accomplishments.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement vouchers for payrolls of casual and JO personnel were submitted to the Audit Team and were duly supported with statement of duties and responsibilities and accomplishment reports were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to submit immediately the remaining DVs and thereafter, observe the due dates of submission of disbursement vouchers and payrolls as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant submitted the remaining DVs and supporting documents. The supporting documents were verified and found complete by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Supporting documents were submitted but some were submitted later than prescribed dates; Audit Team verified and found them complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to assign another personnel who will be responsible in the timely preparation of the disbursement voucher for remittances and remit to the barangays the shares of RPT/CTC and related penalties.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant remitted the shares from RPT and CTC collected to the barangays except the unaccounted balances. With the development, the audit observation is updated and presented anew in audit observation no. 8, page no. 60.
observations.reason: Accounting Office failed to provide the supporting schedules in such detail to properly identify the respective barangays to which the collections belonged; matter being updated in subsequent observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to submit immediately the remaining disbursement vouchers and to observe the prescribed due dates of submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-City Accountant submitted the DVs and supporting documents to the Audit Team within considerable days after due date and the documents were verified and found complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to analyze the Due to BIR account to establish the amount of taxes for refund to the employees and those for remittance to BIR.
observations.managementAction: Withheld taxes were remitted immediately on the following month for which these were deducted by the OIC-City Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the committee to conduct the inventory and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year. The Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office each assign personnel to maintain separate ledger cards for all property and to work on the reconciliation of the accounting and property records with the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in order to establish correctness of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant that a monthly reconciliation between cashbooks and the accounting records be done so that Cash in Local Treasury is accurately presented in the financial statements and in the cashbooks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer to submit to the COA Audit Team of the municipality the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on each findings and recommendations discussed in the CY 2016 Annual Audit Report pertinent to their offices, in compliance with Section 96 of the General Provisions of RA No. 10717, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act of 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances whose purpose has already been served and strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. We also recommended the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, withhold the salaries of all officers and employees with outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted.

We recommended for the immediate submission of the lacking paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance with the aforesaid laws, rules and regulations. Moreover, closely monitor the submission of the reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers for immediate recording in the books of accounts so as to facilitate submission within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the remaining fund balance of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) to the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to the Supreme Court declaration of its unconstitutionality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the City Government. In the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang wPanlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provision of Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Awaiting the Sanggunian Panlungsod, resolution authorizing payment if any claims arises in the future. (Reiterated in Audit Observation No.3, Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of receivables pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason:
Due to unavailability of the necessary supporting documents.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 2, Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC, City Assessor to expedite the conduct of general revision of the Real Property Assessment to optimize the City’s revenue to enable financing of priority projects and improve the delivery of basic services that will benefit its constituents.
observations.reason:
Due to disapproval by the Sangguniang Panglungsod on the submitted budget proposal for general revision.
(Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 11, Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the SDO to submit the required documents and explanations why there are payrolls that bear no approval of the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for evaluation of the Audit Team. Henceforth, require the OIC, City Accountant to ensure that payments are supported with complete documentation and approved by the LCE to establish the validity, propriety, and legality of the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to  return  the  remaining  fund  balance  of  the  Priority  Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) to the Bureau of Treasury  pursuant  to  the  Supreme  Court declaration of its unconstitutionality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the practice of charging expenditures to the Special Education Fund of projects and activities not within the purview of RA No. 5447. Instead, these could be charged to the General Fund of the municipality.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  provide  appropriate organization and composition of LDRRMO in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Report in  compliance  with  Section  12.2  of  the  revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the PESO Manager, in cooperation with the Municipal Treasurer and MSWDO to monitor and exert effort in collecting the loan repayments for the continuance of Overseas Facilitation Fund (OFF) Program to  benefit  more  residents  who seek employment abroad without draining municipality’s fund every year.

We further recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  compute  the interest receivable pertaining to the unpaid balances of OFF loans and record the same to the books of  accounts  in  order  to  reflect  the  correct  balance  in  the financial statements of the municipality.
We recommended the refund the amount  of  P  100,000.00  paid  to  2nd Iloilo Integrated Area Development (2nd IAD), Inc.

We     recommended     that     the     municipality     submit     an explanation/clarification/ justification for the  utilization  of the 20% Development Fund  for projects other than those enumerated  in  the  guidelines.

We further recommended that the  fund  be  budgeted  and  expended  in accordance with proper utilization guidelines  otherwise  it  will  be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-General Services Officer to complete the details of properties in the RPCPPE by securing copies of ARE andcomply with the procedural and documentary requirements for property disposal.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to prepare the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and file the same to the Commission on Audit.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accountant to revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund pursuant to the provisions of Section 98 of PD No. 1445, subject to the authority from the SangguniangPanlungsod (SP) with a condition that appropriation shall be made available in the event that claim arises in the future.
We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to transfer the amount of P583,712.75 to the General Fund and henceforth, comply with DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to review the SLs of Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account by tracing the cause/s of abnormal balances totaling P72,536,883.11 and the City Treasurer to maintain separate account for each special account to facilitate monitoring of the cash balance.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Planning and Development Coordinator and OIC-City Engineer to coordinate with the source agency for the immediate implementation of the projects, if still feasible.
We also recommended to return immediately the excess funds of P1,322,455.14to the source agencies and the fund of P1,000,000.00 for cancelled project to the BTr so that it could be used in other projects or by other agencies needing assistance.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) or the designated program coordinator to monitor the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF to ensure timely completion; and
City Budget Officer to provide specific or itemized projects for PPAs with lumpsum appropriation in close coordination with the end-user.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:
LDRRM Officer to monitor the implementation of the projects that remain unimplemented and take action on the cause/s of the delay or partial implementation; and
OIC-City Accountant to submit immediately the required supporting documents for the disbursements amounting to P1,581,620.00 for post-audit and evaluation.

We recommended that the City Mayor advise the:
LSB Co-Chairmantomaximize the utilization of SEF by identifying specific priority projects and effective monitoring of its implementation to address the actual needs of the schools; and
OIC-City Accountant to submit the required supporting documents for the disbursements amounting toP4,871,104.32 for verification of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) or designated personnel to ensure that complete documents are submitted by the casual and JO personnel in support to their claims, programs/projects are indicated in the payrolls and appointments are supported with statements of duties and responsibilities of the personnel to facilitate validation of their accomplishments.

We recommended that the City Mayor support the OIC-City Assessor to cause the preparation of a Tax Impact Study that will be the basis of the revised schedule of fair market value for mass appraisal of real property units of the City.  The sooner the revision is done, the sooner for the City to realize additional income from the expected increase in real property tax collection.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct theOIC-City Accountant to assign another Accounting personnel who will be responsible in the timely preparation of the disbursement voucher for remittances and remit to barangays the shares of P371,586.09from RPT/CTC andP391,399.24 from penalty on CTC and sanitation violation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to submitimmediately the remaining DVs and hereafter, observe the due dates of submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accountant to analyze the Due to BIR account to establish the amount of taxes for refund to the employees and amount that still for remittance to BIR.

inventory and prepare the Report on the Physical Count  of  Property,  Plant

Treasurer and Municipal Accountant that  a  monthly  reconciliation  between
cashbooks and the accounting records be done so that Cash in Local  Treasury
is accurately presented in the financial statements and in the cashbooks.

reconcile the difference between the book balance of  the  municipality  and
the records of Municipal  Development  Fund  Office  of  the  Department  of
Finance and record any necessary adjustments after the reconciliation.

trace up the unreconciled balance of Due from Other Funds and Due  to  Other
Funds and make necessary adjustment, where appropriate, to  reflect  the  correct
account balances   and   present   reliable   data   on   the   Municipality’s
financial operation as of a given period of time.

the wages of job orders and other  inappropriate  expenses  to  the  Special
Education Fund and adhere to the guidelines provided in  the  DEPED-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.


We further recommended that these expenditures be  charged  to  the  General
Fund of the  Municipality  and  expenses  related  to  Alternative  Learning
System (ALS) must be charged to the funds  provided  by  the  Department  of
Education.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant
to transfer the unutilized balance of QRF  and  DRRMF-MOOE  to  the  Special
Trust Fund and record the transfer to the account  Trust  Liability-DRRM  in
the Trust Fund books at the end of the year.


We also recommended that the municipality strictly adhere to the  guidelines
set by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  Municipal  Accountant
and MDRRM Officer to submit to the COA Audit Team of  the  municipality  the
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of  the  Municipal  Disaster  Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (MDRRMF) on or before the 15th day  after  the
end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all  officials  concerned  to
maximize  the  utilization  of  the  20%  development  fund  allocated   for
implementation of various projects, programs and activities to help  achieve
the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes  of  the
municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the  MPDO/local  development
council to ensure that the fund be budgeted and expended in accordance with
the proper utilization guidelines  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  and  to
establish guidelines for monitoring and evaluation of projects.


We also recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist submit  the  documents
relative to the livestock livelihood program and closely monitor  the  said
projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials concerned  appropriate
1%  of  its  Internal  Revenue  Allotment  in  the  Annual  Budget  for  the
strengthening and implementation of programs,  projects  and  activities  of
Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) in order  to  cater  the
needs  and  uphold  the  rights  and  interests  of  the  children  in   the
municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the  DILG  Operations  Field
Officer assigned to the LGU and the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to  assist
the Municipal Planning Development  Office  (MPDO)  in  the  generation  and
processing  of  sex-disaggregated  data  and  GAD-related   information   to
complement the GAD database.  Likewise, LGU  GFPS  shall  assist  the  Local
Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code or ordinance.


We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue  an  Executive  Order  or
Administrative Order creating  the  M&E  Team.   Further,  the  municipality
adhere to the guidelines on the localization of the  Magna  Carta  of  Women
provided by the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint  Memorandum  Circular  2013-01
and any updates concerning GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned  observe
strictly the rules, regulations and guidelines in LGU transfers/advances  to
LEEs/PUs and consider the options provided in Item 3.4 of  LBC  No.  100  in
the case of its Municipal Economic Enterprise (MEE) operations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all officials concerned  observe
strictly the rules, regulations  and  guidelines  specifically  provided  in
appropriating expenses for Personal Services.

We also recommended  the  refund  of  benefits  received  in  excess  of  PS
Limitation amounting to P 576,954.34.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create  an  Inventory  Committee  to
conduct the inventory and prepare  the  Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the Office of  the
Auditor not later than January 31 of  each  year.  In  the  absence  of  the
General Services Office, the Treasurer’s Office and  the  Accounting  Office
each assign personnel to maintain separate ledger  cards  for  all  property
and to work on the reconciliation of the  accounting  and  property  records
with the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in  order
to establish correctness of the PPE accounts.
This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-007 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendations


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned  officers  and
employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash  advances  whose  purpose
has  already  been  served  and  strictly  adhere  to   the   aforementioned
provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445  and  pertinent  provisions  of  COA
Circular  No.  97-002  and  COA  Circular  No.  2012-004  to  ensure  proper
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.


We also recommended the immediate refund of all unliquidated cash  advances,
otherwise,  withhold  the  salaries  of  all  officers  and  employees  with
outstanding cash advances until the amounts involved are fully restituted.



This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Recommendations


We  recommended  for  the  immediate  submission   of   the   lacking   paid
disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.


We  likewise  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer   and   Municipal
Accountant ensure strict compliance  with  the  aforesaid  laws,  rules  and
regulations. Moreover, closely monitor the submission  of  the  reports  and
supporting documents by the accountable officers for immediate recording  in
the books of accounts so as to facilitate submission within  the first ten
(10) days of the ensuing month.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-011 dated March 14, 2017.

Recommendation


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer  to
return the remaining fund balance of  the  Priority  Development  Assistance
Fund (PDAF) to  the  Bureau  of  Treasury  pursuant  to  the  Supreme  Court
declaration of its unconstitutionality.



This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-004 dated February 10, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct  the  Municipal  Accountant
to:


1. Reclassify the fund transfer from Other MOOE to Due from other LGUs;
2. Monitor and require the liquidation reports from the recipient LGUs;
3. Comply with the requirements provided in COA Circular  No.  2012-001  and
   other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the MPDO submit  copy  of  the  agreed  policy  and
procedures in the monitoring of PAPs and the corresponding report thereon.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-009 dated March 10, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  stop  the  practice  of  charging
expenditures to the Special Education Fund of projects  and  activities  not
within the purview of RA No. 5447. Instead, these could be  charged  to  the
General Fund of the municipality.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-002 dated January 24, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  provide  appropriate  organization
and composition of LDRRMO in accordance with the guidelines  and  provisions
set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and  CSC  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No.  2014-1
dated April 4, 2014.


This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-006 dated February 24, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant  and
the Municipal Planning and Development  Officer  that  the  20%  development
fund  be  charged  for  disbursements  that  are   investment   or   capital
expenditure  in  nature  and  within  the  priority  programs  and  projects
identified in  Department  of  Interior  and  Local  Government  (DILG)  and
Department  of  Budget  and  Management  (DBM)  Joint  Memorandum   Circular
      No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-008 dated March 9, 2017.

Recommendation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct BAC, through the  Secretariat
comply with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the revised Implementing Rules  and
Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-003 dated February 7, 2017.

Recommendation


We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  require  the  BAC  to  prepare  and
submit the Procurement Monitoring Report in compliance with Section 12.2  of
the revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of  Republic  Act  (RA)
No. 9184.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-005 dated February 17, 2017.

Recommendations

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  PESO  Manager,  in
cooperation with the Municipal Treasurer and  MSWDO  to  monitor  and  exert
effort in collecting the loan repayments for  the  continuance  of  Overseas
Facilitation  Fund  (OFF)  Program  to   benefit  more  residents  who  seek
employment abroad without draining municipality’s fund every year.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant  compute  the  interest
receivable pertaining to the unpaid balances of OFF  loans  and  record  the
same to the books of accounts in order to reflect  the  correct  balance  in
the financial statements of the municipality.

This audit observation had  been  communicated  to  the  management  through
Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated March 13, 2017.

We recommended that:

a) the City Mayor and the concerned SDO liquidate fully their cash advances.
Henceforth, ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all
cash advances within the prescribed period in accordance with the above-
cited rules and regulation; and

b) the OIC, City Accounting Office adjust the account Advances to Officers
and Employees to the respective asset accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC-City Accounting
Office to transfer the unutilized balance of the proceeds from sale of bidding
documents to the General Fund by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and
crediting Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current Account in the amount of
P340,948.00.

We further recommended the annual reckoning of excess collections over
payments made for the honoraria and overtime pay of the government personnel
involved in government procurement be undertaken to determine the amount
that should be recorded as income in the General Fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-
City Accounting Office to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request
them to submit documents pertaining to their claims to the City Government. In
the event that they fail to support their claims, revert the account to the
unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the
Sangguniang Panlungsod with a condition that appropriation shall be made
available in the event that claim arises in the future, pursuant to the provision of
Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC-City Accounting
Office to request from the Commission on Audit for the write-off of receivables
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC, City Assessor’s
Office to expedite the conduct of general revision of the Real Property
Assessment to optimize the City’s revenue to enable financing of priority
projects and improve the delivery of basic services that will benefit its
constituents.

We recommended that the:

e City Mayor stop the grant of cash advance for purposes other than those
provided in COA Circular No. 97-002;

e City Treasurer ensure that all payments to suppliers are made through
checks in accordance with the imprest system of disbursements to give
reasonable assurance that funds are fully safeguarded against loss, misuse or
misappropriation; 8

« Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) make sure that the threshold amount for
shopping is complied with and only those that are emergency in nature be
purchased thru shopping;

« Special Disbursing Officers and other accountable/liable officers refund the
amount of P243,300.00 representing excess meals purchased as against the
number of attendees;

o OIC-City Accounting Office to examine thoroughly the liquidation reports
and their supporting documents before recording to avoid possible
suspension and/or disallowances in audit; and

« BAC to submit explanation as to the mode of procurement used and/or
documentary requirements relative to the transaction, such as: a) BAC
Resolution recommending to Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) the use
of alternative mode of procurement and approval by the HOPE of the BAC
Resolution recommending the award of the contract; b) Abstract of Canvass
documents for shopping; and c) Certification as to the completeness of the
project and/or Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the SDO to submit the
required documents and explanations why there are payrolls that bear no
approval of the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for evaluation of the Audit Team.
Henceforth, require the OIC, City Accounting Office to ensure that payments
are supported with complete documentation and approved by the LCE to
establish the validity, propriety, and legality of the transactions.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the refund of the amount of
P178,048.53 for the transactions paid in violation of RA No. 9184 and the
amount of related printing cost which is considered unnecessary. Likewise,
instruct the OIC, General Services Office to adopt competitive/public bidding in
all its procurement activities to provide assurance that the lowest possible price
is obtained from such procurement. Moreover, make sure that the publication
of projects, programs and activities of the City Government is in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a) BAC Chair and Members to submit the documer;tary requirements to the
Audit Team for evaluation to establish the propriety and validity of the
claims;

b) City Budget Officer to refrain from allocating in the regular budget the
honoraria of the BAC Chair and Members and use first the collections from
the sale of bidding documents and in case of deficiency, use the agency
savings; and

¢) OIC, City Accounting Office to ensure that subsequent payments of
honoraria be supported with the required documents to avoid
suspensions/disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

> utilize fully the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the intended
beneficiaries can avail the maximum benefits that may be derived by
observing the timelines of activities involved in the procurement process
thru proper planning to ensure that projects programmed for the year
are implemented; and

instruct the OIC-City Engineer to fast track the implementation of
infrastructure projects funded from the 20% Development Fund for the
benefits of the constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor require the OIC-
City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (CDRRMO) to prepare
LDRRMP taking into consideration the identification of hazards, risks to be
managed, approaches and strategies to be applied, roles and responsibilities of
the LGU officials and coordination of disaster risk reduction and management
in the pre-disaster and post-disaster phases.

We recommended that the City Government submit explanation on the
incurrence of operating and administrative expenses of £1,826,310.84 utilizing
the LDRRMF and account for the procured food supplies intended for relief
goods and non-food supplies totaling £2,996,664.05.

Also, we recommended the full utilization of the LDRRMF thru the
implementation of projects that will address the disaster prevention and
mitigation to strengthen and develop the LGU’s preparedness to natural and
man-made hazards and disasters in accordance with the cited laws, rules and
regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor: (a) provide the
necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to establish their own
MRF and to implement waste segregation at source; (b) strictly enforce and
intensify the information campaign on the no-littering policy among the
residents; and (c) strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and
guidelines under RA No. 9003.
